steps.title: Identify the opening question
steps.content: The passage opens with a direct question: “Why do sand cats purr but lions roar?” This immediately signals that an explanation will follow.

steps.title: Match the structure to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D correctly identifies this structure: the passage poses a question about two species, then presents a possible answer through a researcher's hypothesis about the hyoid bone with supporting anatomical details.
The other three choices misrepresent the passage:
  • A falsely claims the facts weaken the theory (they support it)
  • B invents missing content about habitats
  • C misidentifies purring and roaring as shared behaviors rather than contrasting ones
.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
